name: SY08 White Cream Linear Travertine
topApplication: TV Wall · Foyer · Bedroom · Full Living Wall · Bathroom
bestSegments: Aspirational Pro · Tier-2 · Design Millennials · Heritage
keyStrength: Fills Essential Slot #1 (Classic White + Grey Vein) — dense parallel linear grey veins on warm-white base; the highest-volume lead SKU in the collection
keyWeakness: Linear vein regularity may show repeat across 3+ panels; flat satin surface lacks tactile depth
priorityFix: Develop open-pore matte variant — projected score 88-90 and strongest matte white-stone in collection

name: YYC-SY130 Warm Buff Travertine
finish: Matte — warm and slightly rough surface
topApplication: Foyer · Pooja · Dining Accent · Bedroom · Full Wall
bestSegments: Tier-2 Aspirants · Aspirational Pro · Heritage Buyers
keyStrength: Richest warm tone in the collection — buff-golden cream with grey cloud patches; highest emotional engagement on touch (D6: 4/5); deep Indian sandstone/teak cultural resonance
keyWeakness: Diffuse vein system (no hard vein lines) limits drama score; Bengaluru Japandi market prefers lighter tone
priorityFix: None — release as-is; pair with SY134 and SY08 as the warm travertine family

name: YY-SY134 Warm Cream Travertine
stoneReference: Roman Travertine / Travertino Classico / Ivory Travertine
topApplication: Bedroom · Foyer · Bathroom · Dining · Pooja · Commercial Lobby
bestFor: Universal residential — the safest premium pick
keyStrength: Perfect colour fit (5/5) + perfect versatility (5/5) — soft ivory cream with subtle grey clouds; the highest-volume universal residential SKU; pairs with SY130 and SY08 as warm travertine family
keyWeakness: Subtle veining (D3: 3/5) means low drama; competes on warmth and elegance rather than visual impact
priorityFix: None — release as-is; ideal companion to SY130 (richer) and SY08 (white-linear)

name: Silver Grey Marble — Bold White & Dark Fissure
stoneReference: Bardiglio / Silver Grey / Tundra Grey type
finish: High gloss shown · honed variant recommended
keyStrength: Rare dual-vein system — bold white branches + dark hairline fissure network — delivers depth no single-vein grey panel can match
keyWeakness: Cool tone limits North India / Tier-2 appeal; no honed variant confirmed
priorityFix: Develop honed / matte finish variant to capture fast-growing Japandi + luxury segment

name: Light Grey Travertine — Multi-Vein Large Slab
stoneReference: Silver Travertine / Grey Limestone / Grigio Noce Travertine
finish: Open-pore matte — most tactilely premium finish in batch
topApplication: TV Wall · Foyer · Bedroom · Dining Accent · Bathroom · Commercial Lobby
keyStrength: Best open-pore matte finish in the batch; multiple parallel flowing white veins create a meditative travertine rhythm; pairs with stone-001 as the grey stone duo
keyWeakness: Warm-grey tone has narrower mass appeal than pure cream; parallel vein regularity slightly detectable at room scale
priorityFix: Confirm vein repeat length and provide alternating orientation installation guide

name: London Grey Dark — Bold White Vein
stoneReference: Nero Marquina / Black Galaxy / Grigio Scuro
topApplication: TV Feature Wall · Luxury Bathroom · Commercial Lobby · Foyer Statement
bestFor: Drama accent walls; TV walls; luxury bathrooms
keyStrength: Highest veining drama score (D3: 5/5) — bold white Nero-style vein on charcoal is the most visually arresting surface in the collection; fills Essential Slot #5 (Dark Accent)
keyWeakness: Structurally limited versatility (D5: 2/5) — dark panels have narrow application range in Indian homes
priorityFix: Develop honed matte variant — semi-gloss currently limits specification architect appeal in Mumbai/Bengaluru

name: Light Blue-Grey — Single Crack Vein
stoneReference: Bianca Perla / Silver Cloud / Azul Cielo
topApplication: TV Feature Wall · Home Office · Bathroom · Bedroom
keyStrength: Japandi signature panel — cool ice-grey with single dramatic crack vein; highest Design Millennial score (9/10); the SKU that attracts specification architects
keyWeakness: Cool blue-grey has the narrowest mass-market appeal in the batch; plank format creates a different installation aesthetic from slab panels
priorityFix: None needed — market this as the Japandi hero; create installation guide for plank orientation

name: Cream Marble — CE Effect
bestFor: Secondary room applications; entry-level warm cream
keyStrength: Perfect colour fit (D2: 5/5) — warm cream is India's #2 most demanded stone colour; universally acceptable across geographies and segments
keyWeakness: Very low veining drama (D3: 2/5) and minimal texture (D6: 2/5) — competes on price rather than design premium; outperformed in Slot #4 by the SY travertine family
priorityFix: Reposition as entry-level bathroom/kitchen/pooja tile rather than hero feature panel

name: Grey Taupe — Warm Grey Single Vein
stoneReference: Fior di Bosco / Taupe Grey Limestone / Gris du Midi
bestFor: Transitional buyers bridging warm and cool preferences
keyStrength: Greige bridge — warm grey-taupe occupies the useful middle ground between cream and cool grey; SPC construction gives better dimensional stability than PVC for humid Indian conditions
keyWeakness: Neither warm enough for North India nor dramatic enough for design-forward segment; scores mediocre across all dimensions
priorityFix: Position as a background/secondary panel that pairs with bolder feature panels — not a standalone hero

name: SY20 Pink-Beige Textured Stone
stoneReference: Pink Limestone / Dholpur Pink Sandstone / Travertino Rosa
bestFor: Heritage revival + biophilic briefs; North India market
keyStrength: Highest tactile quality of all warm panels (D6: 5/5) + India-native cultural resonance (Dholpur / Rajasthan pink sandstone); the pink-blush tone is distinctive and deeply embedded in Indian architectural heritage
keyWeakness: No veining (D3: 1/5) caps composite score; pink undertone polarises buyers outside North India; heavy texture limits application range
priorityFix: Market specifically for heritage revival + boutique hospitality briefs; foyer accent wall application

name: Warm Greige — Dual Vein with Copper Hairlines
stoneReference: Diana Royal / Fior di Pesco Carnico / Breccia Aurora
topApplication: TV Feature Wall · Foyer · Bedroom Headboard · Dining Accent
bestFor: Brass / copper hardware interiors; warm contemporary luxury
keyStrength: Unique copper/reddish-brown hairline vein network — directly echoes the dominant brass/copper hardware trend in Indian premium interiors; the only panel in the collection that creates visual coherence with brass fixtures
keyWeakness: Copper tone polarises — conflicts with chrome/steel hardware; requires design confidence; Tier-2 market may find it unfamiliar
priorityFix: Develop leathered or open-pore matte variant — copper veins become tactile and dimensional, elevating score to 82–84

name: Grey Fossil Limestone — Ceppo di Gré Type
stoneReference: Ceppo di Gré / Grigio Ceppo / Pietra Fossile
topApplication: TV Wall · Foyer · Home Office · Commercial Lobby · Dining Accent
bestFor: Specification architects; boutique hospitality; design-forward residential
keyStrength: Highest tactile score in the collection (D6: 5/5) — dense fossil aggregate creates micro-topography that changes character under raking light; the only panel referencing Ceppo di Gré, a globally prestigious Italian specification stone
keyWeakness: Aggregate-texture language is unfamiliar to mass-market Indian buyers; D3 Veining 2/5 caps composite score despite exceptional geological authenticity
priorityFix: Position in architect/designer channel separately from residential channel — use as the collection's credibility SKU with specification professionals

name: Classical Rock — Rough Textured White Cream
stoneReference: Quartzite Rock Face / Natural Cleft Stone / Sandstone Ledger
topApplication: Foyer Stone Feature · Dining Accent · Exterior-Interior Transition
bestFor: Biophilic + boutique hospitality; design-led accent walls
keyStrength: Maximum 3D tactile drama (D6: 5/5) — the highest-relief surface in the collection; lighting-reactive (changes character dramatically between day and night); defines a different product category from marble-look panels
keyWeakness: No veining (D3: 1/5) and very limited versatility (D5: 2/5) — niche application only; Tier-2 market reads rough texture as unfinished rather than premium
priorityFix: Market exclusively to design-forward and boutique hospitality segment; not a residential volume SKU
